news-korea-econ.title: Japanese PM Begins Defense Review amid Growing Threats from China and Russia
news-korea-econ.description:
Japanese Prime Minister Takaichi Sanae on Monday inaugurated a panel to review Japan’s defense posture in a time of growing threats from China, North Korea, and Russia.

news-global-econ.title: Russia built global recruitment pipeline targeting vulnerable migrants for Ukraine war: report
news-global-econ.description: Human rights groups allege Russia recruits tens of thousands of vulnerable migrants from over 130 countries to fight in its ongoing war against Ukraine.

news-global-econ.title: Fed Leaves Interest Rates Unchanged, Saying Economy is Expanding at Solid Pace
news-global-econ.description:
The central bank has maintained its benchmark interest rate target at a range of 3.5 percent to 3.75 percent since its last cut in December. 
The post Fed Leaves Interest Rates Unchanged, Saying Economy is Expanding at Solid Pace appeared first on Breitbart.

news-global-econ.title: Peter Schweizer: 'Political Violence Will Get Worse' Until We Deal with Cancerous Rhetoric
news-global-econ.description:
The shots that rang through the ballroom at the Washington Hilton last week bounced off the same walls as when President Ronald Reagan was shot and nearly killed 45 years ago. But the reaction to the two events shows how much our culture has degraded.

news-korea-soc.title: Global task force dismantles 'pig-butchering' crypto fraud rings preying on Americans
news-korea-soc.description: The FBI and international police forces arrested 276 suspects in a global operation targeting cryptocurrency pig-butchering scam centers overseas.

news-korea-soc.title: Court sentences former world leader to 7 years in prison for resisting arrest, other charges
news-korea-soc.description: Ousted South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol sentenced to seven years in prison after martial law decree that triggered a severe political crisis.

news-korea-soc.title: New IRA bombing fuels fears of global militant network tied to Iran, Hezbollah
news-korea-soc.description: The New IRA reportedly claimed a car bomb attack on a Belfast police station, threatening further strikes amid alleged Iran and Hezbollah connections.
